## Task: Fix [medium] UI/UX: Search input relies only on a placeholder, no accessible name
## Step: write-tests

You are a fix agent. Resolve the following final-review finding.

Review dimension: UI/UX
Severity: medium
File: src/app.ts:69-71
Title: Search input relies only on a placeholder, no accessible name

Finding:
The search input is created with `searchInput.type = 'text'` and `searchInput.placeholder = 'Search...'` but no associated `<label>`, `aria-label`, or `title`. A placeholder is not an accessible name: it is not exposed reliably to assistive technology and disappears as soon as the user types, leaving the field unlabeled. Screen-reader users get no clear purpose for the field. The field construction is part of the modified `app.ts`.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
In `src/app.ts`, in the `startApp` toolbar-building block where `const searchInput = document.createElement('input'); searchInput.type = 'text'; searchInput.placeholder = 'Search...';` is defined, add an accessible name immediately after, e.g. `searchInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Search files');`. Keep the existing `placeholder` as a hint. Alternatively, add a visible `<label>` element and associate it via `for`/`id`. Make no other changes; ensure existing tests (`src/app/render-search.test.ts`, `src/app/toolbar-handlers.test.ts`) still query the input by its current selectors.

## Review Feedback History (please address all items)
Attempt 1: The search input accessible name tests (6 new tests in start-app.test.ts describe block 'search input accessible name') are well-designed and correctly characterize the desired fix. They are specific, focused on one behavior each, cover edge cases (aria-label persistence after typing, placeholder preservation, selector compatibility, widget-move invariance), and use clear descriptions. However, the overall test changes have significant problems: (1) The core implementation fix — adding searchInput.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Search files') to src/app.ts — was NOT applied, so all 4 aria-label tests fail. (2) Numerous unrelated test changes were bundled in: upload control modifications (start-app.test.ts, upload-a11y.test.ts, app.css.test.ts) for a separate keyboard-accessibility fix, and 5 loading indicator tests (render-orchestration.test.ts) for a feature that doesn't exist. These unrelated changes cause 16 additional failures beyond the 4 from the actual task, for a total of 20 failing tests (up from 0 before). (3) The upload test modifications in start-app.test.ts also broke a previously-passing test ('renders an upload control...'). The search input accessible name tests themselves are correct and should be kept, but all out-of-scope test additions should be reverted, and the aria-label fix must be applied to src/app.ts.
